commit.message:
fix(dj): sync button now phase-aligns without jumping to start

The phaseAlign() algorithm was broken — it snapped to the nearest beat
then added the other deck's phase offset, which could seek far from the
current position (often back to 0). Rewrote to compute the minimal time
nudge needed: get both decks' beat phases, find the shortest phase
difference (always ≤ half a beat period), and apply that tiny offset.

Sync button now does BPM match + phase align in one click, ready for
a perfect beat-matched crossfade.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: martig7 <143552868+martig7@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(obs-tests): surface silent skips and harden WebSocket auth

When OBS is not installed, the integration suite was silently skipping
all 16 tests and reporting exit code 0, making failures invisible.

Three changes fix this:

1. obsOrchestration.test.js — print an actionable console.warn with
   install instructions when OBS is not found, so developers see exactly
   why tests were skipped instead of getting a blank result.

2. obsHelper.js — after OBS starts headlessly, read back the actual
   obs-websocket config.json it wrote (some OBS versions regenerate the
   file with auth_required=true and a fresh random password on first run).
   The real credentials are then used for the protocol handshake and
   returned in wsSettings, so tests never fail with an auth mismatch.

3. ci.yml — add an explicit `obs --version` step before the test run so
   the job fails loudly if OBS wasn't installed correctly, and pass
   --reporter=verbose to the test run so skipped tests are surfaced in CI
   output.

commit.message:
fix(ci): prevent stale coverage baseline from persisting across main-branch runs (#8157)

The coverage baseline cache had one stale entry
(`coverage-baseline_22533277814`) containing **46.4%** instead of the
correct ~97%. Two bugs caused this: the bad value was saved without
checking that the coverage gate passed, and the old `workflow_run`
design evaluated `github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'` to `false` for
merge-queue-landed commits (where `github.ref` is
`refs/heads/gh-readonly-queue/...`), so the baseline was never updated
after the initial bad write.

## Changes

- **Require gate success before saving baseline** — both "Save coverage
baseline" and "Cache coverage baseline" now additionally condition on
`steps.coverage-gate.outputs.conclusion == 'success'`, so a failing or
partial run can never overwrite a valid baseline:
  ```yaml
  if: >-
    github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' &&
    steps.merge-coverage.outputs.skipped == 'false' &&
    steps.coverage-gate.outputs.conclusion == 'success'
  ```

- **Fix `github.ref` evaluation** — switching from `workflow_run` to
`workflow_call` means `github.ref` correctly reflects the caller's push
ref (`refs/heads/main`), so the save condition is actually satisfied on
main-branch pushes.

- **Tighten restore-keys prefix** — changed from `coverage-baseline` to
`coverage-baseline_` (trailing underscore) so the prefix match only hits
entries written by this workflow (`coverage-baseline_<run_id>`), never
an accidentally created plain `coverage-baseline` key.

Once merged, the next passing push to `main` will write
`coverage-baseline_<run_id>` = ~97%. GitHub prefix-match semantics
return the newest entry, so the stale 46.4% entry is immediately
superseded and auto-evicted after 7 days unused.

commit.message:
Reduce and gate kinm trace volume (#15)

* Reduce and gate kinm trace volume

Remove the noisiest OTEL instrumentation from kinm and make the
remaining spans opt-in. This drops raw SQL and argument attributes,
removes redundant adapter and DB helper spans, and replaces
high-cardinality list and watch attributes with coarse flags and
counts.

Add KINM_TRACE_LEVEL with off, basic, and verbose levels and default
it to off. Spans in this package now only start when that level
allows them and the incoming context already carries a parent span,
which keeps standalone background usage quiet while preserving HTTP
request traces when enabled.

* Update pkg/db/strategy.go

crypto/spiffe: use Ed25519 for SVID key generation (#145)

* crypto/spiffe: use Ed25519 for SVID key generation

Ed25519 is faster for signing/verifying, produces smaller keys and
signatures, and has safer deterministic signing compared to ECDSA P256.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* Update crypto/spiffe/spiffe.go

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Josh van Leeuwen <me@joshvanl.dev>

* crypto/test: add CRLSign key usage to root CA certificate

Standard CA certificates should include both CertSign and CRLSign
key usage to support certificate revocation list signing.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* Use correct non pointer for ed25519 keys in PEM encoding

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

* crypto/spiffe: don't take pointer of ed25519.PrivateKey

ed25519.PrivateKey is a value type ([]byte), not a struct, so taking
its address creates *ed25519.PrivateKey which is not handled by
EncodePrivateKey's type switch, causing "unsupported key type
*ed25519.PrivateKey" errors at runtime. Use the value directly.

Signed-off-by: joshvanl <me@joshvanl.dev>

commit.message:
fix: Infobox rechts ausgerichtet, Rahmen passt sich Schriftgröße an

Problem 1 – Position:
  panelX war fix auf ImageWidth * 0.04f (links) gesetzt.
  Neu: Panel wird rechtsbündig positioniert.
  Rechter Rand = ImageWidth * 0.04f (4 % Seitenabstand).
  panelX = ImageWidth - rightMargin - panelWidth

Problem 2 – Feste Breite:
  panelWidth war unveränderlich auf 340f gesetzt.
  Neu: g.MeasureString() misst für jede Zeile die tatsächliche
  Textbreite (Titel-Font bzw. Info-Font getrennt). Die maximale
  Breite bestimmt panelWidth = maxTextWidth + 2 * padding.
  Damit passt der Rahmen sich automatisch an FontSizeBase an.

Weitere Verbesserungen:
  - Einheitlicher innerer Abstand (padding = 16f) für alle Zeilen
  - Frühes Return wenn lines.Count == 0 (kein leerer Rahmen)
  - Entfernung der -10-Offsets (Inkonsistenz zwischen Fill und Draw)

commit.message:
Add validation and unit tests AffineGrid (#27572)

### Description
<!-- Describe your changes. -->
This pull request improves the robustness and correctness of the
`AffineGrid` operator in ONNX Runtime by adding stricter input
validation and updating the implementation for better type safety. It
also introduces comprehensive unit tests to verify error handling for
invalid inputs.

### Input validation enhancements

* Added explicit checks in `affine_grid.cc` to ensure that the batch
dimension in `size` matches the batch dimension in `theta`, and that
`theta` has the correct shape for both 2D (`[N, 2, 3]`) and 3D (`[N, 3,
4]`) cases. This prevents silent errors due to shape mismatches.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L116-R126)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L131-R147)

### Type safety and code clarity improvements

* Updated grid generation functions to use the template type `T` for
Eigen vector creation, replacing hardcoded `float` types. This ensures
correct behavior for different data types and improves code clarity.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L31-R36)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-be745903b4daaa5f01d8cf60477899d19d712d88fad2e9b923f0ab3ec36c1c67L50-R60)
* Added clarifying comments to matrix and array initializations in
affine grid generator functions to make the code easier to understand
and maintain.

### Test coverage improvements

* Added a series of unit tests in `affine_grid_test.cc` to validate
input shape checks, including cases for incorrect tensor dimensions,
mismatched batch sizes, and invalid `size` lengths. These tests ensure
that the operator fails gracefully with informative error messages when
given invalid inputs.
* Ensured the required test utility header is included to support new
tests.

### Code cleanup

* Removed unused includes from `affine_grid.cc` to tidy up the codebase.

feat: recursive agent discovery and subdirectory-aware CWD resolution (#522)

* feat: recursive agent discovery and subdirectory-aware CWD resolution

- Agent discovery now recurses into workspace subdirectories (up to 4 levels)
  to find .github/agents/ and .copilot/agents/ directories, matching the
  existing squad discovery behavior
- CWD resolver now derives project root from agent file path instead of
  always using workspace folder root, so agents launched from subdirectories
  (e.g. c:\workspace\alfred\.github\agents\alfred.agent.md) get the correct
  CWD (c:\workspace\alfred)
- Shared EXCLUDED_DIRS constant between agent and squad discovery
- Added tests for recursive discovery and subdirectory CWD resolution

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use cross-platform path separator handling in deriveAgentProjectRoot

path.dirname() on Linux doesn't recognize Windows backslash separators,
returning '.' for paths like 'C:\Users\...\agent'. Use the already-normalized
forward-slash path to find the last separator instead, preserving the
original separator style via substring.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — add .copilot/agents scanning, make sync discovery shallow

- Add .copilot/agents scanning to both scanDirForAgents (sync) and
  scanDirForAgentsAsync (async) so repo-local agents stored under
  .copilot/agents/ are discovered alongside .github/agents/.
- Make sync discoverAgentsInWorkspace shallow (root-level only) to
  avoid blocking the extension host on large monorepos during initial
  activation. Recursive subdirectory scanning is async-only via
  discoverAgentsInWorkspaceAsync used during refresh.
- Move recursive discovery tests to use async path, add test for
  .copilot/agents discovery in workspace subdirectories.

commit.message:
fix(dj): sync button matches BPM only, no longer seeks/resets position

The sync button was calling phaseAlign() which seeks the track to align
beat phases between decks. This caused the track to jump back to the
nearest beat boundary (often position 0), making crossfading impossible.

Now sync only adjusts pitch/BPM to match the other deck, letting the DJ
control positioning manually for smooth crossfades.

commit.message:
DS audit: 14 methodology fixes + docs + charts + 2026 projection

Evaluation
- Replace forward-chain holdout with true LOSO (leave-one-season-out)
- Add 1000-sample bootstrap 95% CI to all accuracy reports
- Add always_team_a, adj_margin_sign, win_pct_sign, lower_seed baselines
- LOSO accuracy: 64.7% (CI 59.3-70.1%), was reported 64.2% via biased method

Label orientation
- Fix ~67% positive-label bias: reorient each row so team_A = higher adj_margin
- Label flipped when orientation swapped; always_team_a baseline now ~52.4%

Feature engineering
- Extract tournament seeds from cbbpy home_rank/away_rank columns
- Add is_tournament binary feature to gate seed signal vs regular-season rows
- Fix conf_strength_tier NaN: D-I teams now get 1.0 (was 0.0 for 83% of rows)
- Implement 2-iteration SOS to break circular opponent-strength dependency
- Remove home_edge (perfectly collinear with neutral_site in tournament data)
- Gate interaction features behind --interactions flag (off by default)

Model training
- XGBoost: max_depth=2, min_child_weight=10, reg_lambda=5.0, reg_alpha=1.0
- Switch XGBoost calibration to isotonic (was sigmoid, designed for SVMs)
- Add --include_regular_season flag with --regular_season_weight=0.3
- build_match_dataset now returns (X, y, meta, weights) — 4 values

Simulation
- Warn when no --bracket_file provided (was silent)
- Auto-select by adj_margin rank (was win_pct rank)
- Consistent team_A orientation in simulate_once and simulate_once_fast
- Fix missing return statements and def main() in simulate_bracket.py

New tooling
- scripts/generate_charts.py: LOSO-by-season, model-vs-baselines, champion-probs charts
- show_results.py: auto-detects latest sim by mtime, accurate metrics
- results/sim_2026_5000.json: 5000-sim 2026 projection (adj_margin selection)
- results/charts/: PNG charts embedded in README

Docs
- README.md: complete rewrite with Mermaid pipeline diagram, methodology section,
  accurate metrics, new CLI flags, known limitations, 2026 season section
- CHANGELOG.md: full sprint entry documenting all 14 fixes
- .github/copilot-instructions.md: new, covers all conventions and new flags
- .gitignore: add project-specific rules (data/processed, models, results JSONs)

fix: add root package.json to partner-portal builder stage

npm workspaces requires the root package.json to resolve the

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(android): fix USB read timeout — 20ms was too short for PS2000 response

readBytes() was passing stepMs=20 as the bulkTransfer call timeout after
the readTimeout refactor. The PS2000B needs up to ~100ms to process a
query and send a response; 20ms caused all reads to time out silently,
returning empty data.

Fix:
- Use callMs=200 as the per-bulkTransfer timeout (device has time to respond)
- Use stepMs=10 for loop pacing
- Elapsed counter now advances by callMs+stepMs per iteration (conservative)
- queryObject timeout raised from 300ms to 800ms for USB round-trip margin
- If readDeviceInfo() returns zero nominals, emit error and close (instead
  of continuing with broken all-zero readings)

commit.message:
fix(dj): eliminate clicks in fader LFO and scratch pattern chops

Replace abrupt setValueAtTime() gain jumps with 1.5ms linear ramps
at every 0→1 and 1→0 transition. Applies to all four fader schedulers:
- Generic fader LFO (1/4, 1/8, 1/16, 1/32 divisions)
- Transformer pattern (4 chops/beat, 40% duty)
- Crab pattern (4 taps/beat, 28% duty)
- 8-finger crab (8 taps/beat, 20% duty)

The 1.5ms ramp is short enough to preserve the tight chop character
while eliminating the discontinuity clicks at each gain transition.
